name: Swiss Chard Empanadas
recipeIngredient: 2 bunches swiss chard, cut into small pieces
3/4 cup Cotija cheese (or aged Romano or Parmesan), grated
1/4 cup melting cheese such as Jack or Asadero, grated
recipeInstructions:
To make dough: In a large bowl combine flour and salt. Add lard and butter. Mix lightly with fingers until the dough forms pea-sized pieces. Stir in ice water and lightly knead until dough forms a ball. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1-2 hours or overnight.
To make filling: Heat olive oil in a frying pan and add diced onion, salt, and pepper. Cook over moderate heat until onions are soft and begin to turn slightly golden. Add chard stems and cook for a minute or two, then add the chard leaves and cook until tender, about 3-4 minutes. Remove from heat and cool. Mix cooled cooked chard with grated cheeses and taste; adjust seasoning.
To assemble empanadas: On a lightly floured board, roll the dough out to 1/8 inch thickness and cut 6 circles about 5 inches each or 12 smaller circles. Place filling onto half of each circle of dough, leaving a border, and wet the edges of the dough with the egg wash. Fold the dough over to enclose the filling. Seal the edges by pressing the top to the bottom with the tines of a fork. Chill at least 30 minutes before baking.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Place empanadas on a baking sheet and brush tops with egg wash and sprinkle with freshly cracked pepper. Cut small slits in the top of each empanada to allow steam to escape. Bake for 30 minutes or until dough is golden. Transfer to a cooling rack to cool slightly. Serve warm or at room temperature.
Yield: 12-4 inch Empanadas
